tipRules.tip: Fish an unweighted Senko on a wide-gap hook across the surface — hopping and skipping like a fleeing minnow or bream; works best on heavily pressured fish that have seen every other lure
Use 30-lb braid for heavy weeds with a 7:1 baitcaster on a 7'3" MH rod; switch to 15-lb fluoro for open water
In open water, use a fast spinning reel with 10-lb braid on a 7' medium-action rod for the surface Senko technique
If a bass chases the surface Senko, just stop it and let it sit — fish will grab it on the fall even though it doesn't look great sinking

knowledge.insight: Cast an unweighted Senko on a 4/0 wide-gap hook and retrieve with a hopping/skipping cadence across the surface — faster than a floating worm but not burning it; the wide-gap hook acts as a keel to keep the Senko running straight and prevents line twist; 3- or 4-inch size works best
The surface Senko technique is a year-round pattern — works whenever there are fish up shallow, and according to this angler there are always fish up shallow
